scope.target: Create the dedicated workflow and evidence-backed pre-analysis for Use Case and Command Handler boundaries
scope.dependency_radius: Canonical standards, prompts, examples, DI, tests, analyzers, and future generated application structure
constraints: Do not rewrite canonical standards before user decisions
Keep Wolverine and other dispatch packages conditional
Do not use sub-agents unless explicitly requested
non_goals: No architecture decision treated as approved during pre-analysis
No broad translation or unrelated cleanup

inputs.architecture_target: Controller depends on an explicit Use Case interface; Use Case and Handler are separate roles
review_findings: Canonical relationship treats Handler as the default Use Case implementation
Controller standards inject concrete Handlers while examples inject IUseCase
Use Case examples combine ICommand, Execute, and forwarding Handle entry points
Prompts mandate Wolverine handlers despite conditional technology policy
Analyzer tests currently accept injected Handlers in Controllers
user_constraints: Create workflow, Git branch, and pre-analysis first
Use Case and Handler are separate independent objects
API Controller injects a Use Case interface
Normal synchronous API behavior does not publish a Command

steps: Read workflow and architecture skill rules
Inventory canonical standards, prompts, examples, guides, and analyzers
Record findings, preliminary target direction, decisions, stages, and validation
validation: Branch base is main merge commit 343ab29
Workflow Markdown and task JSON exist
deferred_items: D1-D12 require user review before standards implementation

results.summary: Opened the dedicated workflow and recorded the initial architecture/context inconsistency baseline without changing canonical standards.
